title: Newswire mobile onboarding: role, topics, regions, and AI summary preview
productContext: Fictional global finance-news mobile app (product name 'Newswire') for iOS and Android. The app is pre-subscription: there is no published subscriber count, no market-coverage percentage, and no AI-accuracy metric. Newswire is news-aggregation-and-reader software only; it does not employ analysts, does not publish original price targets or ratings, and the in-app 'AI summary' is a machine-generated condensation of publisher articles, never an editorial verdict. Onboarding lets a new subscriber pick a role (for example investor, analyst, operator), topics, and regions, then shows a personalized preview before they start a trial. Every URL in this brief is synthetic and uses example.com.
users: new subscriber choosing a role, topics, and regions for the first time on a phone
returning reader re-running onboarding to retune topics and regions after interests change
content curator at a partner organization setting up a shared topic profile for a team
jobs: select a role, a set of topics, and a set of regions without losing progress on a small screen
see a personalized preview that reflects the selected role, topics, and regions before starting a trial
understand what the in-app AI summary is and is not (machine-generated, attributed to sources, not an editorial verdict) before relying on it
start a trial or skip personalization without being pressured or shown fabricated subscriber or accuracy numbers
requiredJourneys: new subscriber completes role, topics, and regions steps in order and reaches a personalized preview that reflects every choice
returning reader re-enters onboarding, changes topics and regions, and sees the preview update before confirming
any subscriber reaches an explanation of the AI summary that states it is machine-generated and attributed to sources, before the preview leans on it
any subscriber can skip personalization and still reach the app home with a clear non-personalized fallback
constraints: Do not claim Newswire employs analysts, publishes original price targets, ratings, or buy/sell recommendations; the app aggregates and presents publisher content plus a machine-generated summary.

The AI summary must be visibly attributed and labelled as machine-generated; do not present it as editorial fact, a Newswire verdict, or a source of record. Every AI summary element must carry an 'AI summary' label and link back to its source articles.
Do not present AI summaries as accurate, authoritative, or guaranteed; the UI must not display any accuracy percentage, confidence score, or 'most reliable' ranking for AI output.
Do not fabricate subscriber counts, market-coverage percentages, source counts, or any metric not supplied in this brief; onboarding is pre-trial and has no published numbers.
Personalization must be reversible: the subscriber can go back, change, or skip any of role, topics, and regions, and skip-personalization must be a first-class path, not a hidden link.
Exactly one primary action (start trial) may lead the preview step; secondary actions (edit selections, skip) must remain reachable without competing with it.
Do not collect or imply collection of payment during onboarding; onboarding ends at trial start, not at checkout.
The product name Newswire is fictional; do not imply affiliation with any real news publisher, exchange, index provider, or rating agency.

mobileRules: role chips are large enough to tap with a thumb in portrait and the selected role stays sticky as the subscriber advances
the back affordance on role-change-pending does not silently discard topic and region selections made in later steps
topic and region pickers stack vertically and each chosen chip remains visible and removable in a single-handed grip
selection-error-no-region surfaces an inline message that names the missing field and keeps prior selections intact
the personalized preview reflects every selected role, topic, and region and each preview card keeps its AI-summary label attached when collapsed
the preview-empty state offers a non-personalized fallback and keeps the skip-personalization path reachable
the explainer states the summary is machine-generated and attributed to sources in the same viewport as the dismiss action
source links inside the explainer open the source article, not a Newswire-authored substitute
